scenario: Zenith Telco runs a Spring Boot connector fleet on Camunda 8.8 against SaaS clusters that handle 9000 jobs per hour. The platform team must point one fleet at a non-production Camunda Cloud tenant by changing the domain part of the default SaaS connection composition while keeping camunda.client.mode set to saas.
question: Which property changes the domain the Camunda 8.8 client connects to so it targets a non-production Camunda Cloud instance?

options.text: Set camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id to the non-production domain so the SaaS connection composition points the Camunda 8.8 client at the alternate environment
Set camunda.client.cloud.region to the non-production domain so the SaaS connection composition points the Camunda 8.8 client at the alternate environment
Set camunda.client.cloud.host to the non-production domain so the SaaS connection composition points the Camunda 8.8 client at the alternate environment
Set camunda.client.cloud.domain to the non-production domain so the SaaS connection composition points the Camunda 8.8 client at the alternate environment

a.text: Incorrect. cluster-id identifies a specific tenant cluster the client connects to; the documented property for switching the SaaS domain to a non-production instance is camunda.client.cloud.domain.

b.text: Incorrect. The Spring Boot starter does not document a camunda.client.cloud.region key; the property for switching environments by changing the domain is camunda.client.cloud.domain.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented key is named domain, not host; camunda.client.cloud.domain composes the default SaaS endpoint and is changed to target non-production Camunda Cloud.

d.text: Correct. camunda.client.cloud.domain is documented as the property to change for connecting to a non-production Camunda Cloud instance via the default SaaS connection composition.

explanation: Under camunda.client.cloud, the domain property names the domain the Camunda client connects to and is the documented knob for switching to a non-production Camunda Cloud instance. The cluster-id property selects a specific tenant cluster but does not change environments.
